_meta.description: AI-overused vocabulary, era-tagged. Single source of truth for lint.py and the generation-time prompt. Word lists rot as models change (delve era gave way to showcasing era); track Wikipedia WP:AISIGNS and Kobak-style corpus studies for updates.

evidence.kobak_2025: Science Advances 11(27) / arXiv 2406.07016 — excess-vocabulary ratios in 15.1M PubMed abstracts: delves r=28.0, underscores r=13.8, showcasing r=10.7
evidence.liang_2024: ICML 2024 / arXiv 2403.07183 — ICLR-review fold-increases: meticulous 34.7x, intricate 11.2x, commendable 9.8x; adjectives = most stable POS signal
evidence.fairbanks_2026: https://www.theatlantic.com/technology/2026/05/how-to-tell-ai-writing/687345/ — cites Max Planck preprint arXiv 2409.01754 (delve, comprehend, boast, swift, meticulous spreading into speech)

hard_ban._comment: Highest measured ratios. No legitimate density argument survives these.

strong_flag._comment: Liang top-adjective/adverb tells + Kobak style verbs. One use = flag; cluster = rewrite.

density_watch._comment: Common words with measured AI excess (Kobak common-set, best-10 marker delta=0.134). Fine individually; a cluster in one paragraph is a tell.

copulative_avoidance._comment: Geng & Trotta via WP:AISIGNS: LLMs drop plain is/are/has >10% and substitute these. Suggest reverting to is/are/has.
